pages.text: The $(bold)frequency system$() lets you "name" Importers/Exporters for easy identification.

pages.title: How to Set Frequency
pages.text: $(li)Hold any item (e.g., Coal) $(li)Right-click Importer or Exporter $(li)The gate is now "named" after that item$(br2)$(bold)Example$(): $(li)Right-click Importer with Coal → "Coal Importer" $(li)Right-click Importer with Iron Ore → "Iron Ore Importer" $(li)Right-click Exporter with Iron Ingot → "Iron Ingot Exporter"

pages.title: Display in GUI
pages.text: When you open PreFab configuration, the dropdown shows: Without frequency: $(li)Unnamed Importer $(li)Unnamed Importer$(br2)With frequency: $(li)Coal Importer $(li)Iron Ore Importer$(br2)Much easier to identify which gate is which.

pages.title: Frequency Item Storage
pages.text: The frequency item is stored in the Importer/Exporter's NBT data: $(li)Doesn't consume the item (you keep it) $(li)Persists when gate is broken and replaced $(li)Can be changed by right-clicking with different item

pages.title: When to Use Frequency
pages.text: Tip: $(bold)Best practice$(): Set frequency BEFORE configuring PreFab faces$(br2)$(bold)Recommended workflow$(): $(li)Place all Importers/Exporters $(li)Name each one with frequency system (Coal Importer, Iron Ore Importer, etc.) $(li)Exit CM dimension $(li)Configure PreFab faces (dropdown shows clear names)
